step_by_step_thinking: Randomization in a clinical trial reduces confounding, decreases selection bias, ensures comparability of two groups, but does not necessarily increase the external validity of the trial itself. Thus, statement D is incorrect because while randomization improves internal validity by minimizing confounders and selection bias, it doesn't directly enhance the ability of the trial to generalize to other contexts unless additional efforts like larger sample sizes or diverse inclusion criteria are employed. Therefore,
raw_response:
Randomization in a clinical trial reduces confounding, decreases selection bias, ensures comparability of two groups, but does not necessarily increase the external validity of the trial itself. Thus, statement D is incorrect because while randomization improves internal validity by minimizing confounders and selection bias, it doesn't directly enhance the ability of the trial to generalize to other contexts unless additional efforts like larger sample sizes or diverse inclusion criteria are employed. Therefore,
\boxed{D}
